hypothesis: If the integrated thermodynamics–cellular automata–machine learning workflow is applied to Ca2SiO4 synthesis within the specified chemical space and 800–1600°C range, then it will identify at least one commercially accessible recipe that minimizes reactant cost while remaining thermodynamically favorable and predicting Ca2SiO4 as the most probable product because the combined modules constrain the search to low-energy, high-likelihood pathways using market-available reagents.
